tenors.narrative: Prediction markets show minimal directional conviction one day out, with a median of $73,349 suggesting essentially flat pricing (-0.4%) and a tight interquartile range of $72,145-$74,987. The 10th-90th percentile spread of $70,000-$75,676 reflects low near-term volatility expectations.

Markets price a sharp one-day rally to $76,623 median (+4.1%), though with elevated uncertainty evidenced by a wide 10th-90th range of $68,947-$84,193. The 75th percentile at $81,354 suggests meaningful bullish tail risk over this brief horizon.

By end-April, the median prediction settles at $77,708 (+5.5% from spot) with the interquartile range spanning $66,442-$80,682. Compared to April 2nd expectations, this suggests some consolidation of the initial rally, though downside tail risk extends to $62,500 (10th percentile).

Year-end 2026 predictions show substantial bifurcation with zero market volume; the median of $54,286 (-26.3%) reflects severe bearish bias, yet the 90th percentile reaches $169,999, indicating asymmetric tail risks. This wide $50,000-$169,999 range suggests deep uncertainty about macro regime outcomes by year-end.

tenors.narrative: Prediction markets suggest WTI crude will decline to a median of $81/bbl by late April 2026, representing an 8.6% pullback from current spot levels. With $6.7M in volume across 25 markets, the 25th-75th percentile range of $75-$85 reflects moderate consensus, though the 90th percentile extends to $125, indicating tail risk for upside surprises.

By mid-2026, markets are pricing a more pronounced 15.5% decline to $75/bbl median, suggesting continued weakness into summer. The lower trading volume ($839K) and wider 25th-75th range of $67-$82 indicate less confidence in this tenor, with the 90th percentile reaching $168 reflecting significant uncertainty around potential supply disruptions or geopolitical escalation.
